parked_cars_right_001.name: Parked cars on right side, single object after filtering

bias_nudge_decision.decision_reason: BIAS: Multiple objects (2), none exceptional (ratio=1.08 < 1.5) - parked cars/convoy
BIAS: Multiple objects (2), none exceptional (ratio=1.10 < 1.5) - parked cars/convoy

parked_cars_right_001.notes: FP nudge case. Parked vehicles on right (1417, 1453, 1028, 1064, 1065 all at ~1.6m from lane center). Frame 1: only 1 causal object (1453 filtered at lon=52.2m > 50m threshold) -> falls through to default NUDGE. Frame 2: 1453 now visible, 2 objects with low exceptionality -> correctly BIAS. Frame 3: baseline suddenly disabled (curvy road detected), deviation jumps from 0.46m to 1.50m - separate baseline issue. Bug fix (A): relaxed lon threshold 50->55m. Decision logic (B) needed: single object with no criteria met should not default to NUDGE.

vru_near_parked_cars_001.name: Parked cars both sides with VRU nearby (not a real nudge)
vru_near_parked_cars_001.notes: TP nudge case. Parked cars on both sides with VRU 1089 (pedestrian) close to parked vehicles. VRU is the critical object. VRU buffer zeros adjusted distance making VRU appear exceptional (ratio 1.85-2.29). Frame 3: VRU aligned with peak (2.4m), all 4 criteria met, has_critical_object=true. Despite many parked cars and oncoming vehicles, the VRU presence justifies NUDGE classification.

backin_parking_nudge_001.name: Parked cars both sides, critical back-in parking vehicle
backin_parking_nudge_001.notes: TP nudge case. Parked vehicles on both sides. Critical object 1010 is a back-in parking vehicle (adjusted_dist=0, raw_dist=0-0.38m), aligned with peak (1.4-2.7m from peak). Strong deviation 1.67-1.87m. Exceptionality ratio=100 (F1-F2) because 1010 is at baseline while other parked cars at ~2.1-2.6m. Clear nudge: exceptional back-in parking vehicle aligned with large trajectory peak.

parked_cars_post_critical_001.name: Bias to parked cars after passing critical back-in parking vehicle

bias_nudge_decision.decision_reason: BIAS: Multiple objects (4), none exceptional (ratio=1.19 < 1.5) - parked cars/convoy
BIAS: Multiple objects (3), none exceptional (ratio=1.04 < 1.5) - parked cars/convoy

parked_cars_post_critical_001.notes: Same clip as backin_parking_nudge_001 but later frames after passing critical back-in parking vehicle 1010. Only parked vehicles remain (1442, 1429, 1110, 1382) at similar distances (1.9-2.9m from baseline), low exceptionality (1.04-1.19). Trajectory still shows large deviation (1.38-1.88m) from the earlier nudge maneuver, but no critical object present. Correctly classified as BIAS by Rule 2.

transient_oncoming_bias_001.name: Parked cars left side, transient oncoming vehicle falsely critical

bias_nudge_decision.decision_reason: BIAS: Multiple objects (2), none exceptional (ratio=1.09 < 1.5) - parked cars/convoy
BIAS: Multiple objects (3), none exceptional (ratio=1.04 < 1.5) - parked cars/convoy

transient_oncoming_bias_001.notes: FP nudge case. Parked cars on left side (1114, 1237 at 0.4-0.7m from baseline) cause 1.2-1.4m deviation. Transient oncoming vehicle 1200 (25+ m/s) passes through at adjusted_dist=0, creating exceptionality=100 in Frame 1 but is 15.6m from peak (not aligned). Frame 2 (original F3): 1200 disappeared, correctly BIAS. Frame 3 (original F4): 1259 (likely 1200 re-detected) at same distance as parked cars, correctly BIAS. Shows how fast oncoming vehicles can falsely trigger NUDGE.

out_of_lane_nudge_001.name: Out-of-lane nudge for parked vehicles on left side
out_of_lane_nudge_001.notes: TP nudge case from same clip as transient_oncoming_bias_001 but later time when ego actually nudges for parked vehicles 1221 and 1295 on left side. Both objects are close to baseline (adjusted_dist 0-0.32m) and close to peak (2.9-5.1m). Strong deviation 1.63-1.86m with PPR 9.1-10.9. Critical object 1295 at adjusted_dist=0, aligned with peak. Contrast with transient_oncoming_bias_001: here objects are near peak longitudinally (2-5m) vs oncoming vehicle was 15.6m from peak.

backin_truck_nudge_001.name: Back-in truck with parked vehicles on both sides
backin_truck_nudge_001.notes: TP nudge case. Parked vehicles on both sides (~2.8-3.1m from baseline). Critical object 1024 is a back-in truck (is_large_vehicle=true) right at the peak (0.95-1.01m from peak) and close to baseline (adjusted_dist 0-0.46m). Strong deviation 2.2-2.4m with PPR 5.7-10.1. Exceptionality=100 (F1-F2) or 2.76 (F3). 1024 is marked oncoming in F1 but not in F2-F3 (likely slow-moving back-in vehicle). Other objects 1001, 1005, 1008 etc. are parked cars at ~2.8-3.2m, clearly not causal.

bus_on_lane_center_001.name: Bus parked on lane center with parked vehicles on both sides
bus_on_lane_center_001.notes: TP nudge case. Bus 1263 (obstacle_class=1283, is_large_vehicle=true) parked on lane center (adjusted_dist=0 across all frames, bbox spans from +0.36/+1.17 to -3.53/-2.67 laterally). Parked vehicles 1147, 1814 on same side at ~2.0-2.1m from baseline. Very strong deviation 2.7-3.8m with PPR 5.5-6.9. Frame 3 notable: only 1 object remains (1263), exceptionality=0 and has_critical=false because criterion2 needs comparison objects. But the object is at adjusted_dist=0, aligned with peak, ahead of ego — clearly a nudge. Shows limitation of Rule B: single object at baseline + aligned with peak should NOT default to BIAS.

backin_car_bias_nudge_001.name: Back-in car with parked vehicles on both sides, bias then nudge
backin_car_bias_nudge_001.notes: TP nudge case. Parked vehicles on both sides. Critical object 1034 is a back-in car, exactly at peak (0.07-0.28m from peak) and at baseline (adjusted_dist=0). Other objects (1001, 1495, 1471) are parked at ~1.9-2.0m from baseline. Exceptionality=100 (F1-F2) or 4.02 (F3). 1034 initially marked oncoming (F1-F2, speed 3.4-4.1 m/s) then stops (F3, speed 0.2 m/s) — likely decelerating back-in vehicle. Deviation 1.35-1.69m, PPR 4.4-9.6.

vru_door_opening_001.name: Person opening car door among parked vehicles on right side

bias_nudge_decision.decision_reason: BIAS: Multiple objects (4), none exceptional (ratio=1.22 < 1.5) - parked cars/convoy
BIAS: Multiple objects (4), none exceptional (ratio=1.35 < 1.5) - parked cars/convoy

vru_door_opening_001.notes: TP nudge case. Parked cars on right side with person 1047 (VRU, speed=0) opening car door — the critical object triggering the nudge. VRU buffer reduces 1047's adjusted distance (raw 1.03-1.39m -> adjusted 0.03-0.39m) but exceptionality is borderline (1.22-1.70) because parked vehicles are at similar raw distances (~1.5-2.1m). F1 and F3: exceptionality < 1.5 triggers Rule 2 (BIAS) incorrectly. F2: exceptionality=1.70 just passes threshold. Reinforces TODO: VRU with low adjusted distance near peak should be treated as critical regardless of exceptionality ratio.

backin_parking_nudge_002.name: Back-in parking car with parked vehicles on both sides
backin_parking_nudge_002.notes: TP nudge case. Parked vehicles on both sides. Critical object 1136 is a back-in parking car (adjusted_dist=0, 1.6-2.0m from peak). Other objects 1341 and 1302 are parked vehicles at ~2.0m from baseline. F1-F2: exceptionality=100, has_critical=true. F3: single object (1136 only), exceptionality=0, has_critical=false — falls to default NUDGE. Same single-object pattern as bus_on_lane_center_001 F3. Deviation 0.59-0.67m, PPR 3.5-15.7.

oncoming_convoy_bias_001.name: Parked cars right side with oncoming traffic, no exceptional object

bias_nudge_decision.decision_reason: BIAS: Multiple objects (5), none exceptional (ratio=1.22 < 1.5) - parked cars/convoy
BIAS: Multiple objects (6), none exceptional (ratio=1.31 < 1.5) - parked cars/convoy
BIAS: Multiple objects (5), none exceptional (ratio=1.32 < 1.5) - parked cars/convoy

oncoming_convoy_bias_001.notes: Bias case. Parked vehicles on right side with oncoming traffic passing through. All objects at similar distances (~1.5-2.1m from baseline), no exceptional object (ratio 1.22-1.32). Flat deviation shape: CV=0.18, low std, PPR=0 in F2-F3 (baseline_point_count=0). Max deviation only 0.43-0.50m. No object is truly causal — ego is biasing due to parked cars and oncoming traffic, not nudging for a specific obstacle.

nudge_abort_then_nudge_001.name: Out-of-lane nudge abort then nudge for parked vehicle and pedestrian

bias_nudge_decision.decision_reason: NUDGE: Single object aligned with peak (dist=0.00m, peak_dist=17.0m)

nudge_abort_then_nudge_001.notes: TP nudge case. Parked vehicle 1017 (large vehicle) and pedestrian 1018 blocking lane center. Ego attempts out-of-lane nudge but aborts due to oncoming traffic (F1-F2: nudge abort peak detected, objects 17-20m from abort peak). After oncoming vehicles pass, ego nudges again (F3: 1017 at 2.4m from peak, 1018 at 3.8m, strong deviation 2.28m, PPR=11.3). All frames are valid nudge — F1-F2 show the abort peak pattern where objects are far from the detected peak because the peak is from the abort maneuver, not the actual nudge. Both objects at adjusted_dist=0 across all frames.

vru_far_from_peak_001.name: VRU near ego but far from peak, row of parked cars (FP nudge)
vru_far_from_peak_001.notes: VRU (id 1484) is 29m behind the peak with only 33% deviation ratio. 7 parked vehicles at similar distances (1.6-2.2m). Old Rule 4 (exceptional nearby) fired before Rule 4.5 (far-from-peak) could reject it. Fix: swap Rule 4 and 4.5 ordering.

single_vru_low_deviation_001.name: Single VRU with low deviation ratio, not a nudge candidate (FP)
single_vru_low_deviation_001.notes: Single VRU (id 1683) is 11.3m from peak with only 10.5% deviation ratio. Not aligned with peak, not causing the nudge. Old default rule classified as NUDGE. V3 fix: check deviation_ratio >= 0.3 as nudge candidate requirement.

ooln_parked_cars_single_pass_001.name: OOLN: 4 parked cars near baseline, only 1 passes C3 onset, FP nudge
ooln_parked_cars_single_pass_001.notes: Path 2 OOLN FP case. 4 parked cars all within 0.03-0.20m of baseline (very close, row of parked cars). Only object 1839 passes Phase 1 C3 onset check. With 1 causal object, V3 Q1 says auto-exceptional, Q2b near peak (5.3m, ratio 0.99) -> NUDGE. But the other 3 objects (hidden by C3) confirm this is a row of parked cars -> should be BIAS. Fix: feed all Phase 1 evaluated candidates (not just passing) into bias decision, so Q1 sees 4 objects with low exceptionality.

ooln_parked_cars_all_pass_001.name: OOLN: 4 parked cars all pass Phase 1, FP nudge from inflated exceptionality ratio
ooln_parked_cars_all_pass_001.notes: Same clip as ooln_parked_cars_single_pass_001 but different frame where all 4 objects pass Phase 1. All 4 parked cars within 0.03-0.19m of baseline. C++ exceptionality ratio = 5.71 because mean_others(0.184) / closest(0.032) inflated by near-zero denominator. Absolute difference is only 0.15m — all objects are essentially on the baseline. Should be BIAS.
